assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_027",
  "is_optimal": false,
  "approve": true,
  "feedback": "Backup and rewrite /etc/resolv.conf with multiple nameserver entries and verify hosts: line includes dns.",
  "concerns": [
    "Overwrites existing resolv.conf, possibly discarding custom options",
    "Static file may be overwritten by NetworkManager or DHCP",
    "DNS server order can affect resolution behavior",
    "Unreachable DNS servers may cause resolution failures"
  ],
  "suggested_improvements": [
    "Preserve existing search, domain, and options lines when generating new resolv.conf",
    "Use the system's network manager configuration instead of editing the static file directly",
    "Validate connectivity to each DNS server before applying",
    "Set correct file permissions (e.g., 0644) after creation"
  ],
  "security_score": 7,
  "best_practices_followed": false
}

The description of the fix is provided; we need to evaluate according to approval guidelines.

The vulnerability: Configure Multiple DNS Servers in /etc/resolv.conf. The scan failed, meaning the fix hasn't been applied? They gave remediation attempt commands. The LLM verdict shows they attempted fix. We need to evaluate quality/optimality.

The fix includes backup, overwrites resolv.conf with two nameserver entries, checks hosts line includes dns. It mentions risks and possible issues.

According to guidelines, imperfect but working fixes should be APPROVED with suggestions.
